title: Telling Your Employer You Need Time Off
summary: Reaching out to an employer during this time is usually simpler than it feels — a short, direct message is enough.
category: After a Loss I: The First Weeks
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Telling Your Employer You Need Time Off | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Reaching out to an employer during this time is usually simpler than it feels — a short, direct message is enough.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Most employers have bereavement leave policies, though terms vary.
A brief, factual message is entirely appropriate — no need to over-explain.
Ask HR about specific leave policies once you're able.

paragraphs: A short message covers what's needed: that a family member has passed away, and that you'll need some time off, with a rough sense of how long if you know it yet. There's no expectation of a longer explanation.

Many employers offer a specific number of bereavement leave days, sometimes with different terms depending on the relationship to the person who died — HR can clarify specifics once you're ready to ask, which doesn't have to be immediately.

nextSteps: Send a brief, factual message to your manager or HR.
Ask about bereavement leave policy specifics when you're able.
Give yourself permission to take the time offered.
disclaimer: Workplace policies vary significantly — your HR department can confirm what applies to your specific situation.
